name: Safe Mode
description: Sometimes hardware and/or software doesn't behave as expected. ESPHome supports a "safe mode" to help recover from repeated boot failures/reboot loops. After a specified number (the default is ten) of boot failures, the safe mode may be invoked; in this mode, all components are disabled except serial logging, network (Wi-Fi or Ethernet) and the OTA component(s). In most cases, this will temporarily mitigate the issue, allowing you a chance to correct it, perhaps by uploading a new binary.

config_entries.label: Boot Is Good After
config_entries.description: The amount of time after which the boot is considered successful.

config_entries.label: Disabled
config_entries.description: Set to `true` to disable safe_mode. [Ota](https://esphome.io/components/ota/) automatically

config_entries.label: Num Attempts
config_entries.description: The number of failed boot attempts which must occur before invoking safe mode.

config_entries.label: Reboot Timeout
config_entries.description: The amount of time to wait before rebooting when in safe mode.

config_entries.label: Storage
config_entries.description: Where to persist the boot-attempt counter. One of:
